entry.title: Upgrade : From SAP BW/4HANA 2.0 Sp10 to SAP BW/4HANA 2023 Version
entry.content:
Hello All,

Our client is planning to upgrade the BW application from : BW/4HANA 2.0 SP10 to BW/4HANA 2023.

We would like to collect the key information's like below as part of our analysis , Could you please help me with the information which will help us for the same.

- Whether we need to migrate it to version 2021 first or directly migrated to version 2023

- What are all the existing functionalities or objects getting impacted with newer releases?

- Any manual/automated adjustments required post upgrade?

- From BW Development perspective - Do we need adjust modelling objects post upgrade ?

 

Thanks,

Kiran

entry.title: Re: Custom Program Invoces Message Output
entry.content:
Did you already check for output Customizing before going to Abap?

  • Setting Up Scheduling Agreement Release Outputs

entry.title: Re: How to get a "Display only" Fiori app from a "Manage" Fiori app
entry.content:
Great post.

How about creating a Behavior Projection and don't expose Create/Update/Delete?

Would that work?

entry.title: SAP CodeJam in Bielefeld, Germany
entry.content:
Hello Thomas,

we would like to host another SAP CodeJam in Bielefeld, Germany.

One (or both) of the following topics would be great:

  • Hands-on with the btp CLI and APIs
  • With SAP Build, Create Apps, Processes, and Business Sites Without Coding

Regarding the dates we are flexible, anything between April and November would work.

We can host up to 25 participants and would organize lunch and snacks. Start would be at 9 am, end would be around 5 pm.

Thank you in advance!

David

entry.title: Re: SAP GUI MFA with SAP Secure Login Service and Microsoft Entra ID
entry.content: Hi @Colt ! Thanks a lot for your feedback. I've attached the source draw.io diagram to the blog post.

entry.title: Re: Rate Elements limit in Subscription Billing.
entry.content:
Hi Stefan,

Thank you for your useful input and support!

Regards,

Shpresim

entry.title: Recently SAP ICC certified? Learn how to leverage your certification benefits effectively:
entry.content:
In today's ever-evolving business landscape, to stand out from the crowd is essential. With competition intensifying and customer expectations soaring, businesses need more than just innovative solutions; they need credibility, visibility, and trust. With SAP Certification – a prestigious endorsement, will opens doors to a world of opportunities. Let's now explore how partners can harness the full potential of SAP Certification to propel their businesses to new heights. 

1. SAP Certificate and Test Report: 

Achieving SAP Certification isn't merely about crossing a milestone; it's about earning a badge of excellence. Upon successful completion of the certification process, partners will receive an official SAP Certificate and a comprehensive Test Report. These documents serve as tangible proof of compliance with SAP's rigorous standards and specifications. The SAP Certificate is a testament to a partner's commitment to quality and innovation, while the Test Report provides detailed insights into the solution's functionalities and technical performance. Partners are encouraged to leverage these assets to enhance credibility, instill confidence, and differentiate themselves in the market. 

2. SAP Certified Logo: 

The SAP Certified Logo is more than just a symbol; it's a beacon of trust and reliability. Endorsed by one of the world's leading software solution providers, this logo carries immense weight and prestige. Upon certification completion, partners are granted the privilege of displaying the SAP Certified Logo across various marketing materials and channels. Whether it's adorning the company website, featuring in promotional materials, or gracing social media profiles, the SAP Certified Logo serves as a powerful endorsement of the solution's quality and integrity. By prominently showcasing this logo, partners can reinforce their alignment with industry-leading standards and instill confidence in customers and prospects alike.

3. SAP Certified Solutions Directory and SAP Store: 

Visibility is crucial in today's crowded marketplace. Fortunately, SAP offers partners two invaluable platforms for increasing exposure and attracting potential customers: the SAP Certified Solutions Directory (CSD) and the SAP Store. By publishing their certified solutions in these platforms, partners gain access to a vast network of customers actively seeking innovative solutions to their business challenges. The CSD serves as a centralized hub for partners to showcase their certified solutions, personalize their entries, and provide links to official SAP resources, thereby reinforcing their credibility and authenticity. Similarly, the SAP Store acts as a virtual showroom where customers can browse, compare, and purchase certified solutions with confidence. With personalized listings and special subcategory placements, partners can significantly enhance their visibility and appeal to prospective customers. 

4. SAP ICC Partner Meetups: 

Connection and collaboration are at the heart of SAP's partner ecosystem. Through SAP ICC Partner Meetups and Partner Day Events, certified partners have the opportunity to connect, collaborate, and stay informed about the latest developments in SAP technology and solutions. These events provide a platform for partners to network with peers, share insights and best practices, and gain valuable insights into emerging trends and opportunities. As a certified partner, you enjoy priority access to relevant SAP products and technical updates, enabling them to stay ahead of the curve and drive innovation within their organizations. With events held in various regions and time zones – both in-person and virtually – participation is convenient and accessible for partners worldwide.

5. Success Stories and Press Release: 

Every success story deserves to be shared, celebrated, and amplified. Through SAP's press release and success story initiatives, partners have the opportunity to showcase their achievements, highlight their impact, and inspire others within the SAP ecosystem. Whether it's a groundbreaking implementation, a transformative solution, or a game-changing partnership, every milestone is worthy of recognition. SAP ICC provides partners with Press Release templates to ensure their certification success reach a wide audience across multiple channels. By sharing their success stories, partners not only enhance their visibility and reputation but also contribute to the collective success and growth of the entire SAP community. 

6. Enhancing your Marketing impact: 

In today's digital landscape, mastering key wording for SEO, leveraging video/written testimonials, and engaging with social media are crucial components of a successful marketing strategy, especially for SAP-certified partners. When optimizing content for search engines, it's essential to go beyond mere keyword stuffing and adopt a balanced approach that incorporates both exact-match keywords and Latent Semantic Indexing (LSI) keywords. By understanding the specific keywords their target audience is searching for, partners can enhance their content's visibility and attract potential customers actively seeking certified solutions. Additionally, showcasing real-life experiences and success stories through customer testimonials, whether in written or video form, helps build trust and credibility. Highlighting the benefits of SAP certification, such as shortened implementation times and cost savings, resonates with prospects and sets partners apart as trusted industry leaders. Furthermore, strategic social media engagement allows partners to amplify their marketing efforts, reach a wider audience, and drive traffic to their websites. Consistency, authenticity, and strategic planning are key to success on social media, as partners share newly created content, testimonials, blogs, and press releases across various channels. By updating social media bios, cover photos, and links to reflect their recent certification with SAP ICC, partners can enhance their credibility and visibility in the digital realm, ultimately maximizing the ROI of their SAP certification. 

For more information on SAP Certification and how it can benefit your business, reach out to: icc-info@sap.com 

© 2024 SAP SE or an SAP affiliate company. All rights reserved. See Legal Notice on www.sap.com/legal-notice for use terms, disclaimers, disclosures, or restrictions related to SAP Materials for general audiences.

entry.title: Re: Upgrade : From SAP BW/4HANA 2.0 Sp10 to SAP BW/4HANA...
entry.content: ++ @kurzdo , @FrankRiesner for the better inputs

entry.title: Security in SAP Analytics Cloud, free learning journey
entry.content:
For those who still haven't put the topic of security on their company's daily agenda, I recommend to have a look at Deloitte`s Global Future of Cyber Survey report. 

Interesting (but not surprising) fact is that executives see cyber playing a crucial role for all digital transformation priorities, especially when it comes to cloud and data analytics. Data analytics in the cloud, in SAP`s terminology this means the SAP Analytics Cloud. 

What is security in SAP Analytics Cloud? In detail, it`s about the understanding of the concepts of SAP Analytics Cloud content security, Creating and Managing users and roles, Securing data, content and objects and Configuring authentication options.

Join our new free Managing Security and Administration in SAP Analytics Cloud learning journey and get  basic knowledge and skills required to be able to successfully secure content and data in SAP Analytics Cloud.

SAP Product Learning Coe Analytics Team

entry.title: Script to start,stop,show status,get HW key easily from terminal
entry.content:
Hi to all.

I came across lately to a request to make a script that easily start, stop, show status and get HW keys easily. So, I made one.

This is a handy script for sap basis persons.

Options

1-Start SAP Instances

You can add and start optional instance numbers.

2-Stop SAP Instances

You can add and stop optional instance numbers.

3-SAP Instance Status

You can show here the status of SAP instances if they are: running, stopped or unknown.

4-Show SAP Instances License

You can show here all instances license. You have to input HANA DB credentials to be able to parse the HW KEY.

 

Go and grab the script on my github

entry.title: How to color or mark some rows with comment dimension comments?
entry.content: Is is possible in SAP SAC to somehow mark the comments entered by the users to the dimension comments column? Mark by a different color for example or a checkbox. The use case could be that the user needs to input their comments in lets say 20 out of 100 rows and they want to mark the rows which are left to be commented.

entry.title: Hello EWM Experts,When I Confirm the Warehouse Order for OBO loading is automatically completed.
entry.content: I have created a wave and released it. After that, the system generated some warehouse orders. When I confirmed those orders using the foreground option in the monitor, the system automatically marked the loading activity as completed. However, this automatic loading is not necessary. Can you suggest how I can avoid this auto-loading? Thank you. Best regards, Anil Kumar"

entry.title: Re: Intermittent Incorrect SSR Redirects (request and response mismatch in server.ts level)
entry.content: Please do not post a question about SAP Web sites (community, SAP Support, etc.) if it's not a question about SAP Web sites! You can use Technology Q&A or any groups.

entry.title: Re: How to activate Goods Receipt Based IV Permanently on...
entry.content:
Dear @shreshtha_pareek,

there are two possibilities where you can maintain this as default value for your purchase orders.

If you are working with Purchasing Info Records, then the GR based invoice verification indicator should be set in the PIR. If your are not working with PIRs, you can set the flag for GR based invoice verification in the purchasing data of your supplier.

The PO item will then take the indicator over either from the business partner or the PIR.

 

best regards

Marius

entry.title: How to extract the comments from the dimension comment column to a strings column?
entry.content:
How to extract the comments from the dimension comment column to a strings column?

The use cases we want are:

1) Some rows need to be commented by the users. We want to write a script which would assess which rows among those which require comments are already commented and which not. This could be accomplished with conditionals basing on e.g. string length, but for that we need to create a column with strings not comments.

2) We want to set up automatic export of all of the comments entered by the users

entry.title: Re: How to receive and invoice bitcoin in SAP - Proof of Concept
entry.content:
Nice blog post and glad to be referenced!

I'm a big fan of BTCPay but in the end ended up running individual BTC, Lightning, LNURL services.

Now all we need is to get BTC to be enabled in a purpose build sub-ledger.  🙂

-Andrew

entry.title: Profit center when creating returns order for another plant
entry.content:
A customer returns goods to another plant than from where it was bought.

During Customer Returns (CBAR) creation we are changing the line-item plant from X to Y. They expect to see the profitability segment data (plant and profit centre) to be updated/redetermined but this is not happening.

 


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Profit center when creating returns order for another...
entry.content:
The system is deliberately set up this way within the copying control customising. It is standard system behavior.

In configuration step: 'Set Copying Control: Billing Document to Sales Document' (102906), the flag 'Dependent Profitability Segment' is set. This means, the Profitability Segment from sales would be inherited to sales – including the attribute : old plant information.

Even in returns order, you use a different plant, but Profitability Segment attributes remain the same - This is to insure consistent margin analysis reporting.

If you create returns order without reference, you will see different behavior.

 

I have highlighted the F1 Help information on this field for your information below:

"Profitability Segment Is Dependent on Preceding Document
Controls whether the profitability segment is determined when a sales document is created or whether the profitability segment is taken over from the preceding document.

Use
The profitability segment should be copied over from the preceding document to the sales document. This ensures a correct revenue recognition in subsequent processes.

Example
You create a sales order. Its subsequent documents are an outbound delivery and a billing document. Later on, you need to create a credit memo request.

In Customizing, you have specified that you want the profitability segment to be dependent on the preceding document.

Since you have created the credit memo request with reference to the sales order, the profitability segment is not determined again, but copied over from the sales order."

entry.title: Re: Has anyone pulled in performance data or data from ot...
entry.content: No we use SF Performance Management, but i've never seen EC workflows pull in data from outside of EC. Is it actually possible to pull in PM data like the overall rating from last year? For example, say you were asked to route a workflow differently for a compensation change within EC(not the comp module) if the target employee received a low performance or potential rating last year. The only way i can think to do this would be to load a custom field in EC for that data to sit

entry.title: Special GL indicator for retention process
entry.content:
Hello experts,

I want to assign different special GL indicator for supplier's security deposit and retention procedures. For security deposit we have 'H' as special GL indicator but for retention there is no special GL indicator. In S4Hana public cloud as we cannot create any new special GL indictor. Kindly suggest how to resolve this.

entry.title: Re: How to exclude users from IPS sync
entry.content: thank you! ill try it out and let you know

entry.title: Re: Cross-product Architecture: Embracing Conway's Law for Better Software Architecture
entry.content: Great description of our new Working Model for managing and amplifying our open source practice at SAP.

entry.title: Re: How to activate Goods Receipt Based IV Permanently on...
entry.content:
Please follow the following simple steps,

BP Master -> Enter Vendor Code -> Select Vendor Role detail -> Select relevant Purchasing Organization -> Purchasing Data tab -> Under Control Data -> Set the flag for GR-Based Inv. Verif

entry.title: Re: show posts in GR that has already been fully recieved
entry.content:
Check PO order quantities and PO history with movement types in transaction code ME23N for your needed status!

Else try MB5S transaction code with your PO

entry.title: Re: MDK Dependecys installer JAVA_HOME is different versi...
entry.content:
If you open a command prompt and run 

javac -version

what does it return?

Is it possible that you also have another java version in the path that points to a different version? 

Might also try moving %JAVA_HOME/bin up to the front of the path.

entry.title: Re: "Modifying SAP EWM Outbound Process: Updating Packing Status at Work Center Completion&quot
entry.content: I have a similar requirement. The picking HU is changing the packing status on the delivery to 'C - Complete'. How can I avoid this behavior? I only want the delivery packing status to be updated when the packing HU has been assigned.  Can you provide more information regarding the BADI you suggested?

entry.title: Re: Intermittent Incorrect SSR Redirects (request and response mismatch in server.ts level)
entry.content: Hello @Sandra_Rossi, what I am asking is SAP Composable Storefront related. And I have double checked, the breadcrumb is showing that my question is in the Technology Q&A. Do let me know if I am still wrong, I will delete the question and try to ask at the right place.

entry.title: Installation of ERS on SAP NW7.4 - AIX
entry.content:
I am installing ERS on AIX 7.3 with Power HA Cluster

Node A contains the ASCS + PAS and Node B will be ERS

The issue is with the instance directory of ASCS and ERS and the mount points at failover time

Node A : ASCS = /usr/sap/<SID>/ASCS00/

Node B : ERS = /usr/sap/<SID>/ERS10/

Both are sitting on /usr/sap/<SID> in respective nodes.

When there is a failover from  Node A to Node B , the mount point /usr/sap/<SID> will overlap on the ERS mount point .

How will both directories for ASCS and ERS coexist on the same node B ?

I believe that for a short while untill the ASCS reads the lock table, the ERS instance should be running on node B . But this is not possible because the ASCS filesystem mounts over the existing ERS filesystem

By default SWPM installs both instances on /usr/sap/<SID>

So what is the solution here ? I need to install ERS on a seperate Filesystem, but it defaults to /usr/sap/<SID>

Any advice

Regards

Warren

entry.title: Re: Re-block documents in GTS that were accidently released
entry.content: Thank you, this worked.

entry.title: Re: show posts in GR that has already been fully recieved
entry.content: You can simply execute ME23N and navigate to the Purchase Order History tab. There, you may find related GRNs and invoice verifications for the PO.

entry.title: Re: Packing status becomes complete after using Pick HU
entry.content: Hi Roopendra - Did you ever get the answer to your question? I have the same issue and cannot find a suitable answer. Thanks so much!

entry.title: Re: Table linking billing document to accounting document
entry.content: The table where you can find the linkage between an invoice and the accounting document is BSEG. Also, you can check ACODCA (universal table).

entry.title: Re: MRP Purchase Requisition date calculation
entry.content: Nice blog Krishna, got better clarity on MRP PR date calculation logic.

entry.title: Re: Campaign Approval Workflow restart after rejection
entry.content:
Hello Daniel,

When using custom conditions for approval workflow, you must set 2 BAdI implementations of Marketing: Campaign Approval Workflow, as described in our online documentation Custom Conditions for Campaign Approval Workflow | SAP Help Portal  as well as in the blog Approval workflow in SAP Marketing Cloud (Part 4) ... - SAP Community:

  • One implementation with option Providing additional conditions for scenarios. This implementation defines the custom conditions under Start Conditions when defining the workflow in the Manage Workflows app.
  • One implementation with option Value evaluation of additional conditions for scenarios. This implementation evaluates the the conditions. Without it, there is no evaluation at all of the values that are set in the condition. It will not work.

See the document CustomConditionsRetrictingApprover attached for an example on how to define custom conditions to avoid the creator of a campaign to approve the campaign.

Best Regards, Isabelle Product Owner SAP Marketing Cloud.

entry.title: Replacing members of one dimension using a second mapping model
entry.content:
Dear Community,
I have a question to ask, is it possible to replace via Data Action one or more members of a dimension using a second model with mapping new member and old member?

Specifically, there would be a first model formed in this way:

DataProductCustomerEntityAccountSell-inPrice
202401DUMMYCLI1SOC1QTY10 
202401DUMMYCLI1SOC1PRICE 20
202401DUMMYCLI2SOC2QTY10 
202401DUMMYCLI2SOC2PRICE 20
202401DUMMYCLI3SOC1QTY10 
202401DUMMYCLI3SOC1PRICE 20
202401ART1CLI1SOC1QTY5 
202401ART1CLI1SOC1PRICE 10
202401ART1CLI2SOC2QTY5 
202401ART1CLI2SOC2PRICE 10
202401ART1CLI3SOC1QTY5 
202401ART1CLI3SOC1PRICE 10
202402DUMMYCLI1SOC1QTY10 
202402DUMMYCLI1SOC1PRICE 10
202402DUMMYCLI2SOC2QTY10 
202402DUMMYCLI2SOC2PRICE 10
202402DUMMYCLI3SOC1QTY10 
202402DUMMYCLI3SOC1PRICE 10

The second model would be formed with a mapping for the product dimension:

DataProductOldProductNewKPITech
202401DUMMYART20
202402DUMMYART20

The desired would be to replace the 'Dummy' product of the first model according to the logic of the mapping proposed in the second model.
The result would then be this:

DataProductCustomerEntityAccountSell-inPrice
202401ART2CLI1SOC1QTY10 
202401ART2CLI1SOC1PRICE 20
202401ART2CLI2SOC2QTY10 
202401ART2CLI2SOC2PRICE 20
202401ART2CLI3SOC1QTY10 
202401ART2CLI3SOC1PRICE 20
202401ART1CLI1SOC1QTY5 
202401ART1CLI1SOC1PRICE 10
202401ART1CLI2SOC2QTY5 
202401ART1CLI2SOC2PRICE 10
202401ART1CLI3SOC1QTY5 
202401ART1CLI3SOC1PRICE 10
202402ART2CLI1SOC1QTY10 
202402ART2CLI1SOC1PRICE 10
202402ART2CLI2SOC2QTY10 
202402ART2CLI2SOC2PRICE 10
202402ART2CLI3SOC1QTY10 
202402ART2CLI3SOC1PRICE 10

I tried a couple of solutions using the MODEL and LINK function, but without success.
I thank anyone who can help

entry.title: Re: Monitor Bank Fees app Camt.086 AFP service Service Codes calculated volume field
entry.content:
Hi Emma,

I have the same issue and could get the correct volumes only by not assigning the service codes in the "Manage Bank" app. Have you got any solution with assignment?

Thanks,

Bulent

entry.title: Re: Diagnostic Agents disconnected after SolMan SPS update
entry.content:
Please check SAP notes 2385361 - How to connect Diagnostics Agent to SAP Solution Manager 7.2 system and 2785611 - Compatible Diagnostics Agent version for Solution Manager 7.2 (Upgrade)

Updating the Diagnostics Agent is always (almost 😀) the right choice, because some security vulnerabilities have been discovered lately on it.

entry.title: Re: tax code filed mandatory in po screen
entry.content: Go to SPRO -> SAP Reference IMG -> Material Management -> Purchasing -> Purchase Order -> Define Screen Layout at Document Level -> Select and double-click on 'Field Selection Key'. Then, double-click on 'GR/IR Control' and flag 'Tax code' as a required entry.

entry.title: Re: tax code filed mandatory in po screen
entry.content:
Thanks for the reply 

This is for the sap public cloud version can u please help me to achive this.

entry.title: Re: Table linking billing document to accounting document
entry.content: Table ACODCA not available ECC. Are you referring to S4/HANA based version?

entry.title: Re: Enhancement spot for MSC1N,MSC2N, MSC3N
entry.content:
Hello all, 

There is another error at the coding. 

The length of variable  l_objky should be extended at least to 32 characters. Then all content of material number, batch number and plant will fit into the key field. This is valid for the "old" ECC world.

At S/4 Hana the length should be least 54 characters, due to increased material number length.
Material number = 40 chars, Batch number = 10 chars & Plant = 4 chars

BR Michael

entry.title: Re: MDK Dependecys installer JAVA_HOME is different versi...
entry.content: Hi bill i put %JAVA_HOME%  first in the path and it works thank you.

entry.title: Align Elements In WebI Like My Oven Timer
entry.content:
BusinessObjects BI 4.3 was released almost four years ago, but my company upgraded from 4.2 just over a year ago and we are still learning and figuring out the particulars of the new interface. We did a subsequent patch a couple of months ago and are now on 4.3 SP03 Patch 8.

I like to align elements (i.e., tables, charts, cells, etc.) relative to other elements just to keep things tidy and so they export to Excel nicely. The simplest way to do that is using the snap lines (I am not sure of the official name) that appear as you move one element near another. This is a new 4.3 feature although I am not sure what SP and Patch.

That results in what I call "static alignment" where the elements are aligned as they exist at that moment, but not as they may grow with data or be subsequently moved manually.

We have been able to do what I call "dynamic alignment" or what is referred to as Relative Position for quite some time like this...

The new feature in 4.3 that is taking me some time to get used to is the "Instant Apply" check box at the bottom. I like having it on, but when I want to go from 3.81 inches to 0.00 inches it takes too long (especially on a report with a lot of data) to repeatedly click the "-" reducing the number of inches in 0.1 increments.

There are a few alternatives...

  • Uncheck the "Instant Apply" check box and adjust with the "-" or "+" button to the desired relative value and check it again.
  • Drag the element close to where you want it to be and fine tune the relative value with the "-" or "+" button.
  • Double-click the relative value and type whatever you want it to be.

I usually use the last one. However, I recently discovered another method purely by accident.

If you click and hold the "-" or "+" button (even with "Instant Apply" checked) the relative value will change in 0.1 increments without adjusting after each increment and speed up after 4 or 5 increments just like when I set my oven timer or alarm clock. When you release your mouse button the relative position will be applied if you have "Instant Apply" checked, check "Instant Apply", or click the "Apply" button.

While writing this up I discovered I can also affect the relative value with my "˄" or "˅" keyboard keys. Once you have your cursor in the relative value field (mouse click or tab into it) it will change in 0.1 increments for each arrow key press and will speed up with no delay of 4 or 5 increments if I press and hold it as it does when clicking and holding the "-" or "+" button. When you move your cursor out of the relative value field the relative position will be applied if you have "Instant Apply" checked, check "Instant Apply", or click the "Apply" button.

This may seem minor, but for someone who likes to minimize switching from my mouse to keyboard or keyboard to mouse like me I will certainly be using this feature.

entry.title: Re: Access the SAP Task Center API using Postman
entry.content: This is great Ivan, A big help, Thank you!

entry.title: Re: Campaign Approval Workflow restart after rejection
entry.content:
I have mentioned that we have already set the two BAdi implementations (SWF_WORKFLOW_CONDITION_DEF and SWF_WORKFLOW_CONDITION_EVAL).

As describe the problem is not the custom condition. The problem is after rejection the workflow does not starts again after clicking on "Submit for Approval".

  1. User 1 creates campaign and clicks on "Submit on Approval"
  2. Campaign Approval workflow starts and correctly sends out approval requests
  3. User 2 gets the request and rejects it.
  4. User 1 edits the campaign after rejection and clicks on "Submit on Approval"
  5. PROBLEM: Campaign status is directly on "Released" with no approval request send out.

entry.title: Hello
entry.content: I am from West Africa and happy to be here, I am hoping to start a new career and knowledge in SAP

entry.title: Re: Campaign Approval Workflow restart after rejection
entry.content:
Dear Daniel,

Did you check the implementation example that I provided?

As I mentioned in an earlier reply, create an incident with the details and the CB user with which we can connect to the system, unless you did create an incident already? I have seen an incident recently that seems about this topic exactly.

Best Regards, Isabelle

entry.title: Re: Explore Datasphere via Excel - options
entry.content:
Hi Cristina,

It's really confusing.  This is how I see:

  • SAP Analysis for Microsoft Office - latest version 2.8 SP21
    • Available for the following Microsoft Office versions:
      • Office 365 (Excel and PowerPoint)
      • Microsoft Office 2021 (Excel and PowerPoint)
      • Microsoft Office 2019 (Excel and PowerPoint)
      • Microsoft Office 2016 (Excel and PowerPoint)
    • Connecting:
      • SAP BusinessObjects Business Intelligence
      • SAP BW
      • SAP BW/4HANA
      • SAP Analytics Cloud
      • SAP Datasphere - We were able to connect SAP Datasphere from SAP Analysis for Microsoft Office 2.8 SP19.
      • SAP S/4HANA Cloud
      • SAP HANA
    •  Licensing:
      • It is not free and is not available to buy by itself.  It is available as part of the Business Objects Business Intelligence suite of tools. 

 

  • SAP Analysis for Microsoft Office, edition for SAP Analytics Cloud - latest version 2.8 SP20
    • Available for the following Microsoft Office versions:
      • Office 365 (Excel)
      • Microsoft Office 2021 (Excel)
      • Microsoft Office 2019 (Excel)
      • Microsoft Office 2016 (Excel)
    • Connecting:
      • SAP Analytics Cloud
      • SAP Datasphere
      • SAP S/4HANA Cloud
      • SAP BusinessObjects Business Intelligence
      • SAP BW
      • SAP BW/4HANA
      • SAP HANA
    • Licensing
      • Included with all SAC licenses and requires SAC credentials to connect to the on premise or cloud data source. 
  • SAP Analytics Cloud, add-in for Microsoft Excel - latest version 2024.06
    • Available for the following Microsoft Office versions:
      • Microsoft 365 for Mac
      • Microsoft 365 for Windows
      • Office 2021 for Windows
    •  Connecting:
      • The connections to SAP Datasphere tenants are created in your SAP Analytics Cloud.
      • The connections to SAP S/4HANA Cloud tenants are created in your SAP Analytics Cloud.
      • The connections to SAP BW systems must be created as tunnel connections in your SAP Analytics Cloud.
      • The connections to SAP S/4HANA systems must be created as tunnel connections in your SAP Analytics Cloud.
    • Licensing
      • You can now choose your preferred interface while having all the benefits of SAP Analytics Cloud with no additional cost. All you need is an SAP Analytics Cloud license.

 

More information please check out Understanding the Various Analysis for Microsoft Office Versions

Regards,
Tuncay

entry.title: Is it possible to change the splitted batch position in Outb. Del. after transferred to EWM?
entry.content:
Hi all,
we are using the embedded EWM in our company.
We created an OUTB DLV based on the sales order with the transaction VL01N. In the OUTB DLV we created the batch splitting for one outbound delivery position. By saving the OUTB Deliv. ist transferred to EWM. In EWM the PRDO has NOT been processed. After that we want to change another batch in the splitted batch item because the entered batch was not the right batch. (User´s mistake) But SAP does not allow to change that. (The whole delivery positions are deactivated)
But when there is no batch splitting for the delivery position we can change the batch as we customized that the delivery can be changed after transferring to EWM when it has not been processed in EWM.
(Customizing path: Logistics Execution - Extended Warehouse Management Intergration - Basic Setup of connectivity - Configure SAP EWM-Specific Parameters - Deliv. Chg = X)

Our question: is there any customizing missing by ourself or SAP does not allow to change the split batch item any more even the position in PRDO is not processed?
Thank you for your answer in advance.
Best regards.
Thao

entry.title: SAP Integration Suite – Access Policies for Integration Packages
entry.content:
As of increment 2401 of SAP Integration Suite, you can define access policies for integration packages. This extension makes the lives of tenant administrators easier who need to manage large numbers of integration packages and selectively restrict access to integration content for  different user groups.

Short reminder of what Access Policies are:

With an access policy, you can protect groups of integration artifacts against undesired access. You define access policies as described in SAP Help Portal under Managing Access Policies | SAP Help Portal.

For example, you can define an access policy for all integration flows that fulfil the condition: name contains the string ‘Read’. As consequence, all integration flows that meet this condition are protected against unauthorized access.

Protection against unauthorized access covers:

  • All operations on the design time artifacts (such like editing, saving, or deploying an artifact, for example)
  • All operations on the deployed runtime artifacts (like restarting an artifact, for example)
  • Data that is processed or stored by the artifacts (like business data stored for monitoring purposes or stored by integration flows in local data stores or variables)

To enable dedicated users to access these protected artifacts, a role needs to be defined in SAP Business Technology Platform (SAP BTP) cockpit that is associated with the access policy (for more information, see the online documentation).

Access policies can be defined for all available integration artifact types such like integration flows, value mappings, and so forth.

Back to the new feature introduced with increment 2401.

When you open the access policy screen in the Monitor > Integrations and APIs section of SAP Integration Suite (Access Policies tile), you now notice that you can also select Integration Package as Type:

Using this new option, you only need to specify one single artifact reference to protect all artifacts of an integration package. In the example above, an artifact reference for the integration package with the name My First Integration Package is defined.

You now also understand to what extent the extension makes the life of the tenant administrator easier, whom we talked about earlier:

If you like to protect all artifacts in a dedicated integration package, you can now define an access policy with one single artifact reference. Before this enhancement, you needed to create an individual artifact references for each integration artifact type separately.

Use Cases

You may be wondering in which cases it makes sense to define access policies for integration packages. Let me point out the following rule of thumb:

OptionUse Case
Define access policy for an integration package …If you like to protect all the artifacts of an integration package (including artifacts of all types).
Define access policy for individual artifact types (for example, integration flows and value mappings) …If you like to protect only few, but not all artifacts of the integration package.

Compatibility with Access Policies for Specific Artifact Types

As said, an access policy for an integration package affects the access to all artifact types contained in the package. However, you can still define access policies for individual artifact types. Now the following can happen: you may want to define an access policy for a specific integration package that contains artifacts for which other, artifact type-specific access policies exist already. What happens in such a case? The message at the top of the dialog provides a clue: for compatibility reasons, existing access policies for individual artifact types will remain intact when you define an access policy for an integration package. Access policies for dedicated artifact types co-exist with access policies on integration package level. Or, phrased differently: When you define an access policy for an integration package that contains artifacts that are also protected by another access policy (for example, by an access policy for a specific group of integration flows), the latter remain valid as well. The message prompts you to check if access policies have already been defined for specific artifacts in your package that you want to protect as a whole.

Let's see how the co-existence of access policies on integration package and on artifact level affects things in a specific example.

Let’s assume that an integration package is protected by one access policy. Furthermore, this integration package contains an integration flow that is protected by another access policy.

To walk you through the example step-by-step, the following two access policies are defined:

Access Policy NameProtects
PackageAccessArtifacts contained in the integration package with the name My First Integration Package
FlowAccessIntegration flows (across all integration packages) with a name that starts with the word Read (matches regular expression ^Read.*)

The tenant has two integration packages with the names My First Integration Package and My Second Integration Package. Both packages contain also integration flows protected by the artifact-related access policy (integration flows with a naming starting with Read).

As a result of this setup, the artifacts are now protected as shown in the following figure:

As we said that access policies protect the specified artifacts – unless a user has a role assigned that is associated with the access policy – we can do the combinatorics with 4 fictitious users with different role assignments:

UserAssigned roleRole associated with access policy*Can access
User1

Role1

Role2

PackageAccess

FlowAccess		All artifacts in all shown integration packages
User2Role1PackageAccess
  • In the package My First Integration Package protected by the package-level access policy: All artifacts
  • In the non-protected package My Second Integration Package: all artifacts, unless they are protected by access policy FlowAccess (for which this user has no corresponding role assignment)
User3Role2FlowAccess
  • In the package My First Integration Package protected by the package-level access policy: integration flows that are protected by the access policy FlowAccess. All other artifacts are protected from this user through the package-level access policy PackageAccess.
  • In the non-protected package My Second Integration Package: All artifacts (because here, this user also can access the artifacts protected by the integration flow-related access policy FlowAccess)
User4(No role assigned)n.a.

Because this user does not have either of the roles, they are subject to the access restrictions defined by both access policies.

As a result, the only artifact they can access is the artifact that is covered by none of the access policies (the non-protected integration flow in the non-protected integration package).

*To be more precise: Role associated with an access policy means: For the Values attribute of the role a string is specified that matches the name of the access policy. For more information on this, check out the online documentation in SAP Help Portal under Creating Custom Roles for Access Policies | SAP Help Portal.

entry.title: Re: Navigating Business Transformation: Capgemini's Expertise in Data and AI Analytics with SAP
entry.content: https://www.sap.com/assetdetail/2024/03/7e5cf13b-b07e-0010-bca6-c68f7e60039b.html

entry.title: Re: March Developer Challenge - CloudEvents: Week 4
entry.content:
Here is my submission 👍

JS with solclientjs:

 

const solace = require("solclientjs");

const solaceConfig = {
    host: "tcps://mr-connection-plh11u5eu6a.messaging.solace.cloud:55443",
    vpnName: "eu-fr-devbroker",
    userName: "solace-cloud-client",
    passWord: "mcrtp5mps5q12lfqed5kfndbi2",
    topic: "dev-challenge/week-4/r00k13d3v/processed",
};

function initSolaceSession() {
    const factoryProps = new solace.SolclientFactoryProperties();
    factoryProps.profile = solace.SolclientFactoryProfiles.version10;
    solace.SolclientFactory.init(factoryProps);

    const session = solace.SolclientFactory.createSession({
        url: solaceConfig.host,
        vpnName: solaceConfig.vpnName,
        userName: solaceConfig.userName,
        password: solaceConfig.passWord,
    });

    session.on(solace.SessionEventCode.UP_NOTICE, function (sessionEvent) {
        console.log("Conectado a Solace.");
        subscribeToTopic();
    });

    session.on(solace.SessionEventCode.CONNECT_FAILED_ERROR, function (sessionEvent) {
        console.log("Conexión fallida a Solace.");
    });

    session.on(solace.SessionEventCode.DISCONNECTED, function (sessionEvent) {
        console.log("Desconectado de Solace.");
    });

    session.on(solace.SessionEventCode.SUBSCRIPTION_ERROR, function (sessionEvent) {
        console.log("Error de suscripción.");
    });

    session.on(solace.SessionEventCode.MESSAGE, function (message) {
        console.log("Mensaje recibido:", message.getBinaryAttachment());
    });

    session.connect();

    function subscribeToTopic() {
        session.subscribe(solace.SolclientFactory.createTopicDestination(solaceConfig.topic), true, solaceConfig.topic, 10000);
    }
}

initSolaceSession();

 

Results:

entry.title: Re: ABAP RAP: Unspecified provider error occurred. See Er...
entry.content:
I had the same problem after some time debugging and without understanding anything, I could find the problem: it is related to the use of %tky

In the projection I have implemented an update augment for association fields that I have in the root entity, my code was this:

I always had the same error.

After a lot of time, I found the location of the problem in the class CL_DSP_DSD_BUFFER

At that point, it is searching in <t_dad_buffer> with the ENTITY key from <s_dad_buffer_line>, which appears to be the same value, and I almost lost my mind:

So I changed the variable viewer to HEX and I finally found the problem:

Value from <t_dad_buffer>:

Value from <s_dad_buffer_line>:

Et voilà! Finally!

So changing my code to use the fields instead of %tky I could make it work:

entry.title: Re: Hello
entry.content:
Welcome @BB2 - if you are new then I would like to share some links with you to help get you started, especially since you hope to start a new career:

  • Beginner Corner
  • Career Corner
  • SAP Managed Tags (subscribe to your topic areas of interest)
  • SAP Community feeds

I hope you enjoy SAP Community! Let us know any time you have questions so that we can point you in the right direction!

entry.title: Re: SAP S/4HANA Asset Capitalization – In house production Process
entry.content:
This post is vaguely similar to this one from 2007: - https://community.sap.com/t5/enterprise-resource-planning-q-a/assets-inhouse-production/qaq-p/2540917

Did you have a question? Otherwise, a blog post is superior to a welcome thread.

entry.title: SAP CAR - Entries of WPUBON in /POSDW/TLOGUS
entry.content:
Hello all,

For WPUBON IDocs are creating entries in /POSDW/TLOGUS table but as there is not status update from ECC back to SAP CAR for WPUBON, I would like to know how this works in automatically clearing the TLOGUS table.

As of now, few are not cleared automatically and I am having challenges investigating that. Please help.

entry.title: Re: How can I write my first blog?
entry.content: Hi @Swarup_Kumar - thank you for reaching out. You have taken the right steps by completing the tour of the SAP Community tutorial 2024. There is a blog review process - where did you post, is it in the Enterprise Resource Planning area? The moderators would have to review blogs in the queue.

entry.title: Re: March Developer Challenge - CloudEvents: Week 3
Re: Cut-over calc. input control doesn't work on Optimize...
entry.content:
Hello Experts, could you please confirm if this functionality (cut over calculation input control/FIND formula) is still not working in the optimized stories? 

I am running a rolling forecast scenario so I would like to optimize the way the cut over data is striggered by the system by setting up a cut over input control. 

See the following instructions for more details: https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_ANALYTICS_CLOUD/00f68c2e08b941f081002fd3691d86a7/3e2ad3b322494a4cad6b1a1ebffe93e1.html#formula%3A-using-the-find-function

It seems this is not working in optimized stories. Is that correct? When this is supposed to be working in optimized stories?

You will find attached the example I tried to process in an optimized story. I used the formula FIND to set the input control, no errors were identified in the formula, however my input control is not created and no error message is triggered by the system. 

You will find attached the details of the steps I followed. I do not see errors o I suspect this functionality is not working properly in the optimized models. Is that correct? 

Don not hesitate to contact me if you have any questions. 

Thanks in advance. 

Regards,

Andres

entry.title: Entries not deleted from /POSDW/TLOGUS
entry.content:
Hi,

We have data being created in table /POSDW/TLOGUS in CAR. The rows are sometimes created with a dummy sales material if the material was not found on the till. This data is successfully sent to ECC (via Idoc) where the dummy material is replaced with a valid material number. The issue we have is that the response back to CAR does not delete the data from /POSDW/TLOGUS, probably because of a material number mismatch?

Does anyone know how to address this issue and insure the correct entries are removed from table /POSDW/TLOGUS in CAR?

entry.title: Create new Key Combination
entry.content:
Hello Everyone ,

Greetings of the day. 

i have to create a key combination (sales office-Material) for "manage free goods-sales" app. Is there any possibility that how can i create this Condition type.

As my client has 800+ Customers and 220+ materials. They give a Product Trade Scheme (Exclusive type Free goods) on Sales office base.

As in System there are 3 Key Combinations are there 1.Customer-material ,2.Sales org/Distr.chl/Price grp/Material & 3.Sales org/Distr.chl/Material.

Please help how can i create/maintain these Conditions

entry.title: Customer & Partner Roundtable for SAP BTP ABAP Environment #11
entry.content:
👉 The call for contributions for one of the upcoming roundtables is open! 

 

If you want to show a demo or share a use case scenario for SAP BTP ABAP Environment send us an email and we will get back to you.
 

Introduction

 

As SAP BTP ABAP environment (aka Steampunk) and ABAP Cloud became more and more popular inside and outside of SAP, there is a high demand for rolling out the latest product news and updates, asking questions, and of course showing demos. 

💡 If you weren’t able to join one of our previous roundtables, you can find the slides presented, recordings, and further references in this GitHub repository.


Meeting Information

When:                                                                      
  • April 23rd, 10:00 - 11:00 AM CET  – Zoom Meeting (please register in advance) 
 
Who:
  • All interested customers, partners, and stakeholders are invited to join and exchange ideas and feedback with others and the product team
  • Steampunk team: Frank Jentsch (Product Lead for SAP BTP ABAP Environment),
  •  Iwona Hahn & Burcu Karlidag 
  • Our guests: Sebastian Werner (Product Manager SAP Fiori elements), Stefan Engelhardt (Architect SAP Fiori elements)

Preliminary Agenda:
  • Product news & updates
  • SAP Fiori elements for ABAP developers incl. demo
  • Q&A
Looking forward meeting you!
 


Check out our SAP Business Technology ABAP Environment page in SAP Community for product updates and upcoming events.

entry.title: Hello
entry.content: I am from West African and new here. I would like to start a new career and knowledge in SAP Technology Consulting

entry.title: Re: Taking Data Federation to the Next Level: Accessing Remote ABAP CDS View Entities in SAP HANA Cl
entry.content: Does this apply to Datasphere as well by virtue of it being a HANA Cloud capability?

entry.title: Scenes from an ASUG Mid-Atlantic Chapter Meeting
entry.content:
This past Friday Fairfax Water hosted an ASUG Mid-Atlantic Chapter meeting.  First I want to thank Gwendolyn Glasner, who set up the meeting, Lauren Milligan, who executed the meeting, Atul Patankar, our ASUG Volunteer who introduced the meeting and all of our speakers:

Special thanks to our sponsors:

Agenda:

8:30

9:00

Breakfast and Registration Open

 

9:00

9:15

ASUG Mid-Atlantic Chapter Welcome

 

9:15

9:45

Intelligent Automation for SAP Transformations: How Automation De-Risks Custom Code Modernization

Jason Narnian, smartShift

9:45

10:00

Networking Break

 

10:00

10:45

ASUG Customer Roundtable Discussion

Patty Brown, ASUG

11:00

11:45

You've Paid For It, So Let's Use It

Kristen Scheffler, SAP

11:45

12:30

Lunch & Learn: ECC TO S/4HANA Conversion Approach

Alex Borokov,  Naviste

12:30

1:15

Plant Tour

 

1:15

1:30

Closing and giveaways

 

After introductions, Jason from smartShift presented:

Clean core is a popular topic this year so interest was high. 

Next, for the first time I can recall, we had ASUG's Patty Brown present at our meeting about the value of ASUG.

Patty reviewed the ASUG member benefits.  She leads the editorial team, producing ASUG First Five.

Patty thanked the volunteers at the meeting.  She was very upbeat and supportive.

Next, the great  Kristen Scheffler presented "You Paid for it, so Let's Use it". 

Kristen covered several areas regarding SAP Support and many attendees told me they were looking forward to her slides.

Source: SAP

I've used these options (try chat first, then if it's too long of a wait, I will call CIC).

I didn't know about this, so I will have to try it out later:

Source: SAP

Source: SAP

Kristen provided an update on SolMan, a product I've used for years.  Many of us will need to decide what to do after SolMan 7.2.

Lastly, Alex from Navisite provided a great session about migrating to S/4HANA

Source: Navisite

I invite you to attend our upcoming Mid-Atlantic Chapter meetings:

  • July 26: Topgolf Baltimore
    Call for Sponsors: https://www.cvent.com/c/abstracts/3edfe457-f452-4be3-8cd2-90aa7ebd2d54
    Link to Register: https://www.asug.com/events/asug-mid-atlantic-chapter-meeting-july-2024
  • September 27 – Lewis Ginter Botanical Gardens, Richmond, Virginia
  • October 25 – Topgolf DC

Sponsor tables:

I *forgot* to take pictures of the food!

Until next time.

entry.title: Can the LMS to Growth Portfolio attribute ratings be configured to only send a higher value?
entry.content: Customer is looking for some flexibility on the LMS to HCM interface for attribute ratings earned via learning.  They might like to only see the history of the learning via transcript as one option.  As another option, they might like for the learning to update the growth portfolio but not if the learner was taking a training tied to a skill they already have that would lower the existing value in the growth portfolio (they don't want to discourage taking a training class just because it is associated with a lower proficiency value). SAP SuccessFactors Learning SAP SuccessFactors platform Talent Management

entry.title: Re: March Developer Challenge - CloudEvents: Week 4
entry.content:
Here is my submission:

const solace = require('solclientjs').debug; // Required for debugging purposes
const dotenv = require('dotenv');

// Load the environment variables from the .env file
dotenv.config();

const SOLACE_TRANSPORT_PROTOCOL = process.env.SOLACE_SMF_TRANSPORT_PROTOCOL;
const SOLACE_HOST = process.env.SOLACE_SMF_HOST;
const SOLACE_PORT = process.env.SOLACE_SMF_PORT;
const SOLACE_USERNAME = process.env.SOLACE_SMF_USERNAME;
const SOLACE_PASSWORD = process.env.SOLACE_SMF_PASSWORD;
const SOLACE_VPNNAME = process.env.SOLACE_SMF_VPNNAME;

const MAX_SLEEP = 1000; // Set your desired sleep duration

function directMessageConsume(session, consumerSubscription) {
    try {
        const topic = solace.SolclientFactory.createTopic(consumerSubscription);

        session.subscribe(topic, true, consumerSubscription, 1000);
        console.log(`Subscribed to: ${consumerSubscription}`);

    } catch (error) {
        console.error(`Error subscribing to topic: ${error}`);
    }
}

async function connectAndConsume() {
    // Initialize factory with the most recent API defaults
    const factoryProps = new solace.SolclientFactoryProperties();
    factoryProps.profile = solace.SolclientFactoryProfiles.version10;
    solace.SolclientFactory.init(factoryProps);

    const sessionProperties = new solace.SessionProperties();
    sessionProperties.url = `${SOLACE_TRANSPORT_PROTOCOL}://${SOLACE_HOST}:${SOLACE_PORT}`;
    sessionProperties.vpnName = SOLACE_VPNNAME;
    sessionProperties.userName = SOLACE_USERNAME;
    sessionProperties.password = SOLACE_PASSWORD;

    const session = await solace.SolclientFactory.createSession(sessionProperties);
    session.connect();

    session.on(solace.SessionEventCode.UP_NOTICE, (sessionEvent) => {
        console.log('Session is up');
        directMessageConsume(session, 'dev-challenge/week-4/spirit2681/processed');
    });

    session.on(solace.SessionEventCode.CONNECT_FAILED_ERROR, (sessionEvent) => {
        console.log('Connection failed to the message router: ' + sessionEvent.infoStr);
    });

    session.on(solace.SessionEventCode.DISCONNECTED, (sessionEvent) => {
        console.log('Disconnected');
    });

    session.on(solace.SessionEventCode.SUBSCRIPTION_ERROR, (sessionEvent) => {
        console.log('Cannot add subscription: ' + sessionEvent.correlationKey);
    });

    session.on(solace.SessionEventCode.SUBSCRIPTION_OK, (sessionEvent) => {
        console.log('Subscription added: ' + sessionEvent.correlationKey);
    });

    session.on(solace.SessionEventCode.MESSAGE, (sessionEvent) => {
        console.log('Message Received: ' + sessionEvent.getBinaryAttachment());
    });
}

connectAndConsume();

Published message:

Received message in the consumer:

Status shown in the application:

entry.title: Re: Sub-Order in SAP Asset Management Public Cloud
entry.content:
Dear Dany R ,

 

I checked internally and on the S/4HANA Cloud Roadmap for EAM and there is no mention of sub-order functionality planned .

 

user status are also not available in S/4HANA Cloud

 

Please follow the KBA 2963059 ( https://launchpad.support.sap.com/#/notes/2963059 ) if you would like it added 

 

Best Regards

Enda

entry.title: Re: MRP Run Process with Delayed sales order with MTS (40) Strategy Group
entry.content: Hi Lalith_Perera , are you using S/4HANA Cloud Public or Private ?

entry.title: Re: Addressing Common Questions and Complaints About the New Platform
entry.content:
Hi @jerryjanda 

I just looked at the HCM page blogs by members today and the Kudo count banner on the right side seems to have been reset to 0 recently. Can we get it back to count all the Kudos on all blog posts made by each member ? I was number 3 and now I'm nowhere to be found. For people working in Professional Services and visibility within SAP, this matters A LOT.

Also, could we make the number of views visible on each blog and not just in the summary page "HCM blogs by SAP" ? I would like people to see (and ideally sort by) the number of views when landing on my profile here: https://community.sap.com/t5/user/viewprofilepage/user-id/20879

Thank you for your help.

All the best

Xavier

entry.title: Is starrocks database supported with SAP Data Services?
entry.content:
• Is starrocks database supported with SAP Data Services?
  • Getting ODBC error when trying to connect to starrocks database from SAP Data Services Designer.

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Is starrocks database supported with SAP Data Services?
entry.content:
Dear Customer,

Please note that in the SAP data services product availability matrix, the Starrocks database is not mentioned which means it is not certified with SAP Data Services.

You can always try configuring the starrocks as source/target in designer using the odbc option and creating a dsn, but we donot guarantee that it will work because its not certified.

  • 1191469 - Where to find a current supported platform matrix (PAM) for Data Services/Information Steward https://me.sap.com/notes/1191469
    • starrocks database is not certified with sap data services

Thankyou

entry.title: Accessing S4HANA tables via an OData connection from a user-portal
entry.content:
Hi Friends,

I am new to OData.

I need to create an OData service (URL) that will allow external users logged in via a portal to access data from an S4 System and pull selected information. 

What are the components, and steps? Is there a port involved? Is there a token involved?

If someone can provide a step by step help, that would be great.

Any help would be highly appreciated.

Best,
RS

entry.title: Automatic counter SAC
entry.content:
Hello colleagues,

I need your help. Currently, I'm working on a financial dashboard. The client needs a button to automate the export process as a PDF, and an automatic counter that counts how many times the dashboard has been exported. Could you please assist me in implementing this requirement? Thank you in advance. #sapanalyticscloud #sac

entry.title: Re: Alternative Reference Number required in Document type ZA or Vendor document display
entry.content: What is the config path for field status variant where can bring in Alternative Reference Number

entry.title: Issues with Property Naming Conventions in Service Consumption Model Leading to Filter Errors
entry.content:
Hello SAP Community,

I am encountering a peculiar issue while working with a Service Consumption Model in my SAP BTP, ABAP environment. I have imported metadata from an on-premise endpoint (that I'm connecting to) that utilizes camelCase notation in its properties (example: <Property Name="OrderNumber" ... />). However, when the Service Consumption Model is generated, it automatically converts these names into a format separated by underscores in the generated files, and this is causing me filtering issues.

Here is what happens, step by step:

  1. The original property in the endpoint metadata and the CDS abstract entity (created in BTP ABAP afterwards) is named using camelCase, for example, "OrderNumber".
  2. After importing this metadata into my Service Consumption Model, the property is transformed to "order_number" in the generated model class structure (referenced in the Service Consumption Model).
  3. When I attempt to use this property in a filter (using the Fiori preview app), the condition name within my query class filter implementation is automatically converted to uppercase, resulting in "ORDERNUMBER", without the underscores.
  4. Unless I change the filter condition name to "ORDER_NUMBER", it throws an error, stating that the property "ORDERNUMBER" does not exist.

For reference, my situation closely resembles the examples provided in the following SAP sample exercises:

  • RAP Workshops - Exercise 3
  • RAP OpenSAP - Week 5, Unit 2

Has anyone faced a similar issue or have any insights on how to ensure that the filter works correctly regardless of the notation? I am particularly looking for a solution or workaround that would allow the service consumption model to accurately interpret the property without causing errors in the filter conditions. I would also like to maintain the camelCase notation for the properties in the service binding created based on the CDS abstract entity.

If any further information is required, please ask. Thank you in advance for your assistance and insights!

Rafael

entry.title: Is PPM supported in S/4 PPDS
entry.content:
Hello Experts,

 

We're currently use PPMs instead of PDS's for our PPDS functions in APO. We're wondering if PPMs are still supported when we upgrade our ECC6 to S/4.

 

Thanks,

Damin

entry.title: How to log a pdf and csv file in CPI which is incoming
entry.content:
I am building a scenario in cpi where csv and pdf files are coming to CPI and being sent to receiver.
before processing the files need to be logged in CPI in original format.
Can someone please help me?

@MortenWittrock can you help please?

entry.title: Re: SAP CPI to Microsoft SharePoint Adapter Integration
entry.content:
Hi Alex,

This is informative blog. 

What would be the corresponding steps on Microsoft's end to complete this setup (creation of secret, token, granting access to specific sharepoint, etc). 

 

Thanks,

Puneet

entry.title: How do I find all WebI documents or schedules using OneDrive as a source or destination?
entry.content:
Question:

Any idea how to identify all webi documents containing query(ies) sourced from OneDrive?

Details:

We're using SAP Business Objects Version : 14.3.3.4562.3 (4.3 SP3 P3) in a multi-node/clustered environment.  We've enabled the tokens for saving/sourcing data to/from OneDrive.  This has worked well for about 90 days and we're finding tokens are beginning to expire.  A SAP KB article has indicated multi-node environments this can occur and requires a config change to ensure the excel services run on the same node as the AuthToken saved (See Note  https://me.sap.com/notes/3117434).  However, the next patch we are moving to doesn't appear to have this issue.  We're in a period of lockdown where we will not make updates to a production environment due to increased risk exposure of outages.  Therefore we're wanting to check to see how big of a problem we may have.  For this period we may take preventative steps by having users Delete/Re-add their Auth Tokens ever 30-60 days.  But to do so I need to identify all webi documents, schedules, publications which make use of resources from OneDrive. 

The following Query Builder query looks like it identifies all the times OneDrive is used as as a "Destination".

SELECT TOP 10000 SI_SCHEDULEINFO.SI_DESTINATIONS, *
FROM CI_INFOOBJECTS
WHERE SI_SCHEDULEINFO.SI_DESTINATIONS LIKE '%Microsoft%'

However I'm struggling with identifying all the times oneDrive is used as a "source"

I created a new webi document with a known Excel data source and evaluated the CI_INFOOBJECTS to see if I could find any attribute which would indicate oneDrive as a source.  I found nothing aside from the SI_WEBI_DOC_PROPERTIES indicating the document was sourced from a .xlsx; but there's nothing to say that isn't on the webserver itself. 

Any idea how to identify all webi documents containing query(ies) sourced from OneDrive?

entry.title: Re: Credit Line Analysis App is showing busy indicator.
entry.content:
Hi Poornima,

 

have you checked the F12 console log? Are there any errors logged? Can you screenshot the console log, when you navigate into the app?

 

Best,

Edrilan Berisha

SAP S/4HANA Cloud Financials Development

entry.title: Re: After Update BI 4.3 SP03 Patch 6 Error running the pr...
entry.content:
This happened to us, upgrading from BI4.3 SP02 Patch 5 to Patch 12.  We opened a support incident but after several calls and sending log files, we were no closer to solution.

We eventually figured it out.  Although the documentation does say that the service account must have the "Revoke a process level token" permission, it was never actually required before (unless impersonation was used [I think]), but it is required now with the changes to program object authentication in Patch 10.  We gave our service account this permission, and our program objects started running again.

entry.title: How to Configure New Regions in SAP S/4HANA Cloud?
entry.content:
How to Configure New Regions in SAP S/4HANA Cloud?

entry.title: Is there any remote FM to get list of Serial Number using Material Number ?
entry.content: I found out that TCodes IQ03 and IQ09 can retrieve the list of serial numbers using Material, but I haven't been able to find any related RFC that functions in the same manner. Please help! Thank you!

entry.title: Re: BTP Trial Account - Cannot access ABAP Cloud project...
entry.content:
Hi Armin,

Thanks for your suggestion and detailed explaination. Unfortunately I have already tried it and it did not work. I noticed similar behavior after I added indentity management while working with AWS (Open SAP course). 

For now I have decided to create a separate sub-account for SAP Build Apps and keep my ABAP development and Build development separate, and hopefully avoid such conflicts in future.

Once again thanks for your help.

Regards

ASM

entry.title: Could anyone refer a good SAP implementation service?
entry.content:
We are looking for a good SAP implementation/migration service with reasonable charges.

Appreciate your help.

entry.title: Re: Special GL indicator for retention process
entry.content:
Hi Nitin,

 

SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition has a way to manage Supplier retentions through the following steps:

  • Create the Purchase order (PO) and select the retention type in the Payment Processing tab in the header. After doing this then you can see the retention percentage in the line item detail in the Invoice Tab.
  • After the PO has been created then do the Goods Receipt (If applicable)
  • Then create the invoice for the PO. Make sure you add the “Retention due” date. When you do this you’ll get the warning message about the retention amount. If you haven’t configured the account you’ll need to create the account determination.

 

Please see the screenshots with the key steps/fields representing the process below.

 

If this doesn’t support what you’re expecting please feel free to add more details so the community can continue trying to help.

Ernesto Cepeda

entry.title: “Could not sign in, something went wrong, try again or contact your administrator “
entry.content: Count not sign in. After Registration at code , and after entering email I’d and password,

entry.title: Re: [Problem]: <SID> is not a consolidation target
entry.content:
hi @symon_braunbaer while wondering around I just stomped on your question

It looks exactly as mention in https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-q-a/system-not-in-consolidation-target/qaq-p/9446497 

in the transport route settings, do you see the changes made in domain_controller?

in the transport route settings, did you create a target group consolidation?

if not, give it a try . if yes, can you paste here a screenshot of the stms routes seen in your training system?

cheers!A

entry.title: Re: Could anyone refer a good SAP implementation service?
entry.content:
hi @Sky1 if you ask the sales of sap partners they are all good 

if you ask me, they can be all crap or good depending on expectations

my best answer is the more you/your company will be transitioning the business processes to be compatible with sap erp standard processes and the easier you will find a good service

hope this helps! cheers.A

PS.:if i missed the meaning of your Q let me know and I'll refine my answer

entry.title: Re: March Developer Challenge - CloudEvents: Week 4
entry.content:
I have a problem receiving messages. The code I am using is a sample from GitHub.

https://github.com/SolaceSamples/solace-samples-nodejs/blob/master/src/basic-samples/TopicSubscriber.js

Subscribing to the topic is successful, but I cannot receive messages when I publish events to `

/dev-challenge/week-4/MioYasutake/notification`.

In the monitor app I can see my messages have been processed. I have also tested with other participants' code, but the results were the same.

entry.title: SAP Crystal Reporting 2016
entry.content: HI I am a new user and am having issues when trying to run a report I keep getting messages asking me to choose a data source selection and then to log in, I have no idea what to do is there anyone who can help?

entry.title: Re: Dropdown on Account dimension property in SAC Story
entry.content: Hi Nikhil, there is Only DropDown_1 with property. Updated my question with more specific details. Let me know if I missed something
